blocks.text: H-001 Code Review Report — Final Quality Gate

---
feature-id: H-001
stage: final-quality-gate
agent: engineering-code-reviewer (orchestrator)
verdict: Changes-requested
must-fix-count: 2
should-fix-count: 2
last-updated: "2026-06-29T00:10:00+07:00"
---

- **Hotfix:** H-001 — Fix backend startup failures (module M-001)
- **Severity:** High
- **Pipeline path:** Tech-lead → Dev → QA → Reviewer (BA/SA skipped as hotfix)

### Files Reviewed On Disk

| Fix ID | File | Lines Reviewed | Status |
|--------|------|---------------|--------|
| FIX-1 | `src/main/resources/application-local.properties` | Full file (23 lines) | Read ✅ |
| FIX-2 | `src/main/java/com/hanghai/kchtg/accesslog/scheduler/LogStatsScheduler.java` | Full file (46 lines) | Read ✅ |
| FIX-3 | `src/main/java/com/hanghai/kchtg/config/RolePermissionSeeder.java` | Full file (256 lines) | Read ✅ |
| FIX-4 | `src/main/java/com/hanghai/kchtg/seeder/DataSeeder.java` | Full file (288 lines) | Read ✅ |
| FIX-5 | `src/test/java/com/hanghai/kchtg/orgunit/service/OrganizationServiceTest.java` | Full file (371 lines) | Read ✅ |

### Supporting Artifacts Read

- `_state.md` — hotfix state, severity, stages-queue
- `patch-brief.md` — patch summary (incomplete — many `[CẦN BỔ SUNG]`)
- `tech-lead/04-plan.md` — execution plan with evidence and assessments
- `dev/05-dev-w1-verify.md` — developer verification (BUILD SUCCESS, static-only)
- `qa/07-qa-report-w1.md` — QA verification with runtime findings (DEFECT-1 Critical)

---

The QA Engineer (wave 1) executed **runtime verification** by running `mvn spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=local` and discovered a critical defect that neither the tech-lead plan nor the dev verification caught:

### DEFECT-1 (Critical) — Detached Entity Crash at Runtime

The application **partially starts** (8.226 seconds to "Started KchtgApplication"), `RolePermissionSeeder` (Order 1) successfully seeds 8 roles with ~22 permissions, and `DataSeeder` (Order 2) seeds categories and icons. But **`DataSeeder.seedUsers()` crashes** with:

```
org.hibernate.PersistentObjectException: detached entity passed to persist: com.hanghai.kchtg.user.entity.Role
```

**Root cause:** The `User` entity at `User.java` line 87 defines:

```java
@ManyToMany(fetch = FetchType.EAGER, cascade = CascadeType.PERSIST)
@JoinTable(name = "user_roles", ...)
private Set<Role> roles = new HashSet<>();
```

When `DataSeeder.seedUsers()` retrieves the `Role` via `roleRepo.findByCode("ROLE_SYSTEM_ADMIN")` and adds it to a new `User`, then calls `userRepo.save(admin)`, Hibernate's `CascadeType.PERSIST` on `User.roles` attempts to `persist()` the already-persisted Role → **crash**.

**Why FIX-4's `@Transactional` does NOT solve this:** The Role was persisted in `RolePermissionSeeder`'s `run()` method (which has no `@Transactional`), so it becomes **detached** after that transaction commits. The `@Transactional` on `seedUsers()` only affects the current transaction boundary — it cannot reattach an entity committed from a **different** transaction.

**The comment on DataSeeder.java line 186** — "cascade PERSIST on it is a no-op" — is **incorrect**. Cascade PERSIST does attempt to persist, which causes the crash.

**Fix options recommended by QA:**
(a) Add `@Transactional` to `RolePermissionSeeder.run()` so the Role remains managed when DataSeeder re-fetches it
(b) Change `User.roles` cascade to `CascadeType.MERGE`
(c) In `seedUsers()`, use `entityManager.merge(adminRole)` before adding to user
(d) Make both seeders participate in the same transaction

### DEFECT-2 (Major) — Process Gap

Both tech-lead plan and dev verification assessed FIX-4 as "Correct ✅" based solely on **static code review** (presence of `@Order(2)`, `ROLE_SYSTEM_ADMIN` code, `@Transactional`). Neither ran `spring-boot:run` to confirm the application actually starts. QA is the first stage to discover this runtime failure.

---

**File:** `src/main/resources/application-local.properties` (23 lines)

**Lines verified:**
```
Line 22: # Disable data.sql — use RolePermissionSeeder + DataSeeder instead (they handle seeding at @Profile("local"))
Line 23: spring.sql.init.mode=never
```

**Assessment: Correct ✅**
- Disables Spring Boot's `data.sql` auto-execution in the local profile only
- The `-local` suffix correctly scopes this property
- The project has dedicated seeders (`RolePermissionSeeder` and `DataSeeder`) that handle RBAC seeding

**Limitation:** This only affects the `local` profile. The `dev` profile in `application.yml` (line 97-99) still sets `spring.sql.init.mode=always`, which will execute `data.sql` and fail.

---

**File:** `src/main/java/com/hanghai/kchtg/accesslog/scheduler/LogStatsScheduler.java` (46 lines)

**Lines verified:**
```
Line 32: @Scheduled(cron = "${LOG_STATS_CRON:0 0 3 * * ?}")
```

**Assessment: Correct ✅**
- `0 0 3 * * ?` is a valid 6-field Spring cron: seconds=0, minutes=0, hours=3, day=*, month=*, dayOfWeek=?
- Runs daily aggregate at 03:00 as documented in Javadoc (lines 15-16, 30)
- Externalized via `${LOG_STATS_CRON:...}` — supports runtime override
- Exception handling catches and logs without failing scheduler (lines 41-44)

**Full codebase scan confirmed:** All 4 other `@Scheduled` annotations are also valid:
- `LogCleanupScheduler.java:33` — `"0 0 2 * * ?"` ✅
- `BackupService.java:244` — `"0 0 0 * * SUN"` ✅
- `VerificationTokenService.java:121` — `fixedRate = 3600000` ✅
- `SchedulerConfig.java:14` — Javadoc only ✅

---

**File:** `src/main/java/com/hanghai/kchtg/config/RolePermissionSeeder.java` (256 lines)

**Lines verified:**
```
Line 33: @Order(1)
Line 34: @Profile("local")
Line 149: // Permissions will be saved automatically via @ManyToMany cascade when saving roles.
Line 228: roleRepository.saveAll(roles);
```

**Assessment: Correct ✅ (but with important runtime caveat)**
- `@Order(1)` ensures execution before `DataSeeder` (Order 2)
- `@Profile("local")` confines to local profile only
- Explicit `permissionRepository.saveAll()` removed — permissions saved via `@ManyToMany` cascade
- Idempotent: checks `existsByCode()` for each role (lines 57-62)
- All 8 roles defined with proper permission assignments
- **Runtime impact:** RolePermissionSeeder runs successfully in local profile (verified by QA). However, its `run()` method has **no `@Transactional`**, which contributes to DEFECT-1 in FIX-4.

---

**File:** `src/main/java/com/hanghai/kchtg/seeder/DataSeeder.java` (288 lines)

**Lines verified:**
```
Line 45: @Component
Line 46: @Order(2)
Line 47: @Profile("local")
Line 171: @Transactional
Lines 183-186:
  Role adminRole = roleRepo.findByCode("ROLE_SYSTEM_ADMIN")
      .orElseThrow(() -> new IllegalStateException("Role not found: ROLE_SYSTEM_ADMIN"));
  // adminRole is managed by the transactional context — cascade PERSIST on it is a no-op
```

**Assessment: INCOMPLETE ❌**

The annotations and role code are correct. However, the fix does NOT resolve the startup failure:

- `@Order(2)` correctly follows `@Order(1)` — ✅
- `@Profile("local")` restricts execution — ✅
- `@Transactional` on `seedUsers()` — **does not solve the detached entity issue** — ❌
- `ROLE_SYSTEM_ADMIN` matches `RolePermissionSeeder` definition — ✅

**Critical runtime failure (DEFECT-1):** When `userRepo.save(admin)` is called, Hibernate's `CascadeType.PERSIST` on `User.roles` attempts to re-persist the already-committed Role entity → `PersistentObjectException`. The `@Transactional` on `seedUsers()` cannot fix this because the Role was persisted in a **different transaction** (RolePermissionSeeder's `run()` has no `@Transactional`).

**The comment on line 186** — "cascade PERSIST on it is a no-op" — is **factually incorrect** based on runtime evidence. Cascade PERSIST does attempt to persist, which causes the crash.

**Recommended fix:** Add `@Transactional` to `RolePermissionSeeder.run()` so Role entities remain managed within the same transaction boundary as DataSeeder.

---

**File:** `src/test/java/com/hanghai/kchtg/orgunit/service/OrganizationServiceTest.java` (371 lines)

**Lines verified (all `setCoefficient()` calls):**
| Line | Call |
|------|------|
| 90 | `request.setCoefficient(BigDecimal.valueOf(1.5));` |
| 305 | `request.setCoefficient(BigDecimal.ZERO);` |
| 320 | `request.setCoefficient(BigDecimal.valueOf(-0.5));` |
| 334 | `request.setCoefficient(BigDecimal.valueOf(1.50));` |
| 366 | `unit.setCoefficient(BigDecimal.valueOf(1.0));` |

**Assessment: Correct ✅**
- All 5 occurrences use `BigDecimal.valueOf()` or `BigDecimal.ZERO`
- `java.math.BigDecimal` import present (line 23)
- `UpdateOrgUnitRequest` import added (line 4)
- `mvn test-compile` BUILD SUCCESS confirms zero compile errors

**Test execution:** Cannot run due to Java 25 / ByteBuddy incompatibility (pre-existing environment issue, not related to FIX-5). The compile fix is sufficient evidence.

**Test coverage:** BR-013 (unique code), BR-014 (delete guard), BR-015 (approval workflow), BR-016 (circular ref), BR-017 (coefficient validation) — comprehensive business rule coverage.

---

rows: FIX-1, FIX-2, FIX-3, FIX-5 correct. FIX-4 partially correct (code annotations right, but runtime failure not addressed).
FIX-4's `@Transactional` does not solve the detached entity issue. Root cause is `CascadeType.PERSIST` on `User.roles` combined with non-transactional RolePermissionSeeder.
FIX-1 (clear comment), FIX-2 (externalized cron), FIX-3 (documented cascade), FIX-5 (correct type). FIX-4 (misleading comment on line 186).
Seeders are `@Profile("local")` only. Hardcoded password `admin123` and plaintext token only in local dev. Prod uses PostgreSQL.
Seeder idempotency (`count() > 0` guard) is correct. Circular dependency prevention (`@Lazy`) correct. But cascade PERSIST + separate transactions = crash.
FIX-5 compiles cleanly. Tests cannot run (Java 25/ByteBuddy). Business rule coverage (BR-013 to BR-017) is good.
FIX-3 line 149 comment correct. FIX-4 line 186 comment "cascade PERSIST on it is a no-op" is factually incorrect.
Local profile safe. Dev profile (`application.yml` line 97-99) still sets `spring.sql.init.mode=always` — will execute `data.sql` and fail.

blocks.caption: No secrets exposure risk in production profile. All seeders are `@Profile("local")` only.
rows: `application.yml` uses `${ENV_VAR:default}` pattern — acceptable for local, requires env override for prod.

blocks.text: Open Execution Questions from Tech-Lead Plan

rows: ⚠️ Partially addressed. Production path safe (PostgreSQL + `ddl-auto: validate`). Dev profile still problematic.
❌ Not addressed. File still present at `src/main/resources/data.sql` (54 lines).
✅ Resolved. Full scan confirmed all 5 `@Scheduled` annotations valid.
`DataSeeder.seedUsers()` crashes with `detached entity passed to persist: Role`. Fix: add `@Transactional` to `RolePermissionSeeder.run()` or use `entityManager.merge()` on Role.
Application starts fully — all seeders complete, admin user seeded, no crash.
`application.yml` dev profile sets `spring.sql.init.mode=always` — `data.sql` will execute and fail. Fix: add `spring.sql.init.mode=never` to dev profile or remove `data.sql`.
Dev profile starts successfully or `data.sql` removed.
`src/main/resources/data.sql` still exists — dead artifact referencing non-existent `role` column.
`application-local.yml` (line 16: `spring.sql.init.mode=always`) conflicts with `application-local.properties` (line 23: `never`). Properties wins, but fragile.
Consolidate local profile into single file format.
Future `CommandLineRunner` beans may break ordering without documented convention.
Tests cannot execute — Mockito mock instrumentation fails on Java 25.

items: Remove or archive `data.sql` — dead artifact referencing non-existent schema column.
Consolidate local profile configuration — choose either `application-local.yml` or `application-local.properties`, avoid mixing formats.
Document `@Order` convention for all `CommandLineRunner` beans to prevent future race conditions.
Fix DEFECT-1 before release — add `@Transactional` to `RolePermissionSeeder.run()` (simplest fix, minimal risk).
Resolve Java 25/ByteBuddy compatibility for test execution in CI pipeline.
Add CI verification with production deployment profile (not just `local`).

blocks.text:
**All 5 code fixes are technically correct in their annotations and compile cleanly:**

| Fix | Code Annotations | Runtime Result |
|-----|-----------------|----------------|
| FIX-1 | `spring.sql.init.mode=never` ✅ | Local profile safe ✅ |
| FIX-2 | 6-field cron valid ✅ | Scheduler will run correctly ✅ |
| FIX-3 | `@Order(1)`, removed `permissionRepository.saveAll()` ✅ | Roles seeded successfully ✅ |
| FIX-4 | `@Order(2)`, `ROLE_SYSTEM_ADMIN` ✅ | **CRASH at runtime** ❌ (DEFECT-1) |
| FIX-5 | `BigDecimal.valueOf()` ✅ | Compiles, tests cannot execute (env issue) |

**Three of five fixes (FIX-1, FIX-2, FIX-3) are complete and safe.**

**FIX-5 (test fix) compiles cleanly — test execution blocked by pre-existing Java 25/ByteBuddy environment issue.**

**FIX-4 is INCOMPLETE — the `@Transactional` annotation on `seedUsers()` does not solve the detached entity problem. The application crashes at runtime with `detached entity passed to persist: Role` when `DataSeeder.seedUsers()` adds a fetched Role to a new User and calls `userRepo.save(admin)`. This is a **Critical** defect that prevents application startup.**

**Two additional must-fix items:**
1. `application.yml` dev profile still sets `spring.sql.init.mode=always` — `data.sql` will execute and fail in dev profile (PROF-001)
2. `data.sql` file still on disk — recommendation to remove or archive (should-fix)

**No security, reliability, or deployment-risk blockers identified beyond DEFECT-1.** All seeders are `@Profile("local")` confined. The production path (via `prod` profile) uses PostgreSQL with `ddl-auto: validate` and does not reference `data.sql`. The hotfix commit is atomic and reversible.

**However, the critical runtime defect (DEFECT-1) means the hotfix CANNOT be released as-is.** A backend-developer fix for the detached entity issue is required before release.

blocks.code: Changes-requested high FIX-1: Correct for local profile — spring.sql.init.mode=never disables data.sql (line 23 verified) FIX-2: 6-field Spring cron 0 0 3 * * ? valid — all 4 other @Scheduled annotations also valid (full scan confirmed) FIX-3: @Order(1) present, permissionRepository.saveAll() removed, RolePermissionSeeder seeds 8 roles successfully FIX-4: @Order(2) and ROLE_SYSTEM_ADMIN correct, BUT crashes at runtime with "detached entity passed to persist: Role" — @Transactional does not solve because Role persisted in separate seeder transaction. DEFECT-1 Critical. FIX-5: All 5 BigDecimal fixes compile-clean — mvn test-compile BUILD SUCCESS (625+95 files, zero errors) Build: mvn clean compile test-compile = BUILD SUCCESS (625 main + 95 test, 12.2s) Startup: Application starts in 8.226s, RolePermissionSeeder succeeds, DataSeeder.seedUsers() crashes (DEFECT-1) Profile gap: application.yml dev profile still sets spring.sql.init.mode=always — data.sql will execute and fail (PROF-001) docs/hotfixes/H-001-backend-startup-fixes/reviewer/08-review-report.md docs/hotfixes/H-001-backend-startup-fixes/qa/07-qa-report-w1.md DEFECT-1DataSeeder.seedUsers() crashes with "detached entity passed to persist: Role" — User.roles has CascadeType.PERSIST, Role entity persisted in separate seeder transaction (no @Transactional on RolePermissionSeeder.run()). Fix needed: add @Transactional to RolePermissionSeeder.run() or use entityManager.merge() on the Role in seedUsers(). Severity: Critical — prevents application startup. PROF-001application.yml dev profile sets spring.sql.init.mode=always — data.sql will execute and fail. Required action: add spring.sql.init.mode=never to dev profile section OR remove data.sql entirely. Owner: backend-developer.

title: H-001 Code Review Report — Final Quality Gate
